_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**Biosecurity compliance infrastructure (road freight, Queensland operations):**
The workspace must maintain operational awareness of all active Queensland biosecurity zone boundaries, particularly fire ant, varroa mite, banana, cattle tick, electric ant, and polyphagous shot-hole borer zones. This requires: (a) a current zone map integrated into dispatch/routing systems for Queensland road freight; (b) driver briefing protocols covering movement restrictions and the obligation to notify DPI upon observation of regulated matter; (c) cargo acceptance screening for prohibited/restricted matter categories. The replacement Biosecurity Regulation (to follow the 29 May 2026 sunset review consultation) may alter zone boundaries, movement conditions, and livestock traceability record requirements — the workspace should schedule a system and procedure review once the replacement instrument is made and its commencement date published. Current gap: no evidence that fire ant biosecurity zone routing compliance is systematically embedded in Queensland road freight dispatch. *Source: Biosecurity Regulation 2016, Queensland DPI, current as at 27 April 2026 (updated prohibited/restricted matter lists). https://www.legislation.qld.gov.au/view/pdf/inforce/current/sl-2016-0075*

**Trust governance and record-keeping (Queensland trust structures):**
If the workspace holds or administers any Queensland trust arrangement, it must immediately update internal governance frameworks to reflect: (a) codified trustee duties (good faith, care and skill, prioritising beneficiary interests, maintaining proper records) now set out on the face of the Trusts Act 2025; (b) expanded trustee powers of an absolute owner (subject to the trust deed); (c) new eligibility rules barring minors, insolvent individuals, and disqualified persons as trustees; (d) updated thresholds (capital maintenance/education/advancement now up to AUD 100,000 without court approval). Record-keeping systems must capture decision-making rationale, distributions, and beneficiary communications to the statutory standard. The Trusts Regulation 2026 sets transitional operational detail. Current gap: trust deed review and governance documentation update has likely not been triggered for pre-existing Queensland trust structures. *Source: Trusts Act 2025 (Qld), commenced 28 April 2026; Rose Litigation analysis, 28 April 2026. https://www.roselitigation.com.au/knowledge-centre/queenslands-new-trusts-act-what-trustees-beneficiaries-and-advisors-need-to-know/*

**Nature conservation and environmental cargo documentation (Queensland inbound/outbound):**
The Environmental and Other Legislation Amendment Regulation 2026 (in force 22 May 2026) reclassified conservation status for one fauna species and ten flora species under the Nature Conservation (Animals) Regulation 2020 and Nature Conservation (Plants) Regulation 2020. The workspace's cargo acceptance and customs documentation workflows for live animals, plants, biological specimens, and related materials moving to/from Queensland must reflect updated species classifications. A one-time audit of the relevant Schedule entries against cargo manifests for fauna/flora shipments is required. No system rebuild is anticipated, but existing checklists must be updated. *Source: Environmental and Other Legislation Amendment Regulation 2026, Department of the Environment, Tourism, Science and Innovation, Queensland, commenced 22 May 2026. https://environment.qld.gov.au/management/policy-regulation/changes*
